title: Is Evangelion a Shinjis dream
wikitext:
[]: Is Evangelion a Shinji's dream?             Quote Post by azudem » Mon Dec 04, 2023 2:15 pm What is more real? World 1: - Post-apocalypse - You live in a underground city-fortress - You pilot a giant robot and fight against angels - You easily survive a close nuclear bomb explosion that only flipped your car - 0.00000001% probability events became your everyday routine - You usually save the world in the last available second, like in a cheap movie - You live with most popular girl in school that kisses you because of boredom - Your penguin pet reads a newspaper World 2: - You live in a normal city, no apocalypse or something - You have a normal family - You study in a normal school, and Misato is a class teacher - Asuka is your childhood friend - Rei is a new girl in school, not a clone of your mother with an angel soul - Pen-pen is your alarm clock So, maybe the world in the episode 26 is actually real, and the rest of anime is just a Shinji's dream?

[]: Re: Is Evangelion a Shinji's dream?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 04, 2023 5:34 pm When I first watch episode 26, I felt that I was about to be cheated by a "but it was all a dream, even if it did contain realizations about life" type of ending. That turned out not the case; but some while later I encountered a take that NGE was best read with the NERV/Eva/Angel bits as Shinji having a psychotic break in between periods of normal school and (foster-)home life.

Article: Is Evangelion a Shinjis dream
[]: Is Evangelion a Shinji's dream?             Quote Post by azudem » Mon Dec 04, 2023 2:15 pm What is more real? World 1: - Post-apocalypse - You live in a underground city-fortress - You pilot a giant robot and fight against angels - You easily survive a close nuclear bomb explosion that only flipped your car - 0.00000001% probability events became your everyday routine - You usually save the world in the last available second, like in a cheap movie - You live with most popular girl in school that kisses you because of boredom - Your penguin pet reads a newspaper World 2: - You live in a normal city, no apocalypse or something - You have a normal family - You study in a normal school, and Misato is a class teacher - Asuka is your childhood friend - Rei is a new girl in school, not a clone of your mother with an angel soul - Pen-pen is your alarm clock So, maybe the world in the episode 26 is actually real, and the rest of anime is just a Shinji's dream?

[]: Re: Is Evangelion a Shinji's dream?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 04, 2023 5:34 pm When I first watch episode 26, I felt that I was about to be cheated by a "but it was all a dream, even if it did contain realizations about life" type of ending. That turned out not the case; but some while later I encountered a take that NGE was best read with the NERV/Eva/Angel bits as Shinji having a psychotic break in between periods of normal school and (foster-)home life.
